fsm_model.description: Complete FSM for TLS connection establishment in Matter, covering version negotiation, certificate validation, cipher suite selection, hostname verification, and optional client authentication according to RFC 8446 and related specifications

states.name: Initial
states.description: Node has not initiated any TLS connection. No endpoint configured.

states.name: Endpoint_Configured
states.description: TLS endpoint (hostname and port) has been configured. Ready to initiate connection.

states.name: Handshake_Initiated
states.description: TLS handshake started. ClientHello sent to server with supported versions and cipher suites.

states.name: Version_Negotiated
states.description: TLS version 1.3 or later successfully negotiated with server. Earlier versions rejected.

states.name: Cipher_Suite_Negotiated
states.description: Cipher suite with Perfect Forward Secrecy negotiated. Non-PFS suites rejected.

states.name: Server_Certificate_Received
states.description: Server certificate received during handshake. Pending validation.

states.name: Certificate_Format_Validated
states.description: Server certificate format validated: X.509v3 compliant and DER encoded.

states.name: Certificate_Chain_Validated
states.description: Certificate chain verified to trusted TLS Root CA Certificate using Chain of Trust.

states.name: Certificate_Expiry_Validated
states.description: Certificate validity period checked. notBefore and notAfter within acceptable range.

states.name: Certificate_Revocation_Checked
states.description: Certificate revocation status checked via CRL or OCSP. Certificate not revoked.

states.name: Hostname_Verified
states.description: Server certificate hostname matches requested TLS hostname. Identity confirmed.

states.name: Client_Certificate_Requested
states.description: Server requested client certificate for mutual authentication. Node preparing client certificate.

states.name: Client_Certificate_Sent
states.description: Client certificate sent to server. Awaiting server validation.

states.name: Client_Authenticated
states.description: Server validated client certificate. Mutual authentication complete.

states.name: Session_Keys_Derived
states.description: Session keys derived using negotiated cipher suite with PFS. Encrypted communication ready.

states.name: Connection_Established
states.description: TLS connection fully established. Encrypted bidirectional communication active.

states.name: Connection_Failed_Version
states.description: Connection failed due to unsupported TLS version. Server proposed version < 1.3.

states.name: Connection_Failed_Cipher
states.description: Connection failed due to unsupported cipher suite. Server selected non-PFS cipher.

states.name: Connection_Failed_Certificate
states.description: Connection failed due to invalid server certificate. Chain validation, format, or expiry check failed.

states.name: Connection_Failed_Hostname
states.description: Connection failed due to hostname mismatch. Server certificate does not match requested hostname.

states.name: Connection_Failed_Client_Auth
states.description: Connection failed due to client authentication failure. Server rejected client certificate.

states.name: CSR_Creation_Requested
states.description: Node needs to create Certificate Signing Request for client certificate. Preparing CSR.

states.name: CSR_Created
states.description: Certificate Signing Request created in PKCS#10 format. Ready to send to CA.

states.name: Application_Data_Transmission
states.description: Connection fully established and actively transmitting encrypted application data. Node communicating with TLS server.

states.name: Connection_Closing
states.description: Connection is being gracefully closed. close_notify alert sent, awaiting peer acknowledgment.

states.name: Connection_Closed
states.description: TLS connection successfully closed. All resources released, session terminated.

states.name: Certificate_Revocation_Check_Failed
states.description: Revocation check could not be completed due to service unavailability. Policy determines whether to fail-open or fail-closed.

states.name: Connection_Failed_Network
states.description: Connection failed due to network-level errors (DNS failure, connection timeout, network unreachable).

transitions.timing_requirement: Chain validation within reasonable CPU time
Immediate abort if client cert required but unavailable
Client certificate sent within handshake timeout
Server Finished message received within handshake timeout
CSR creation within reasonable CPU time

actions: validation_error_details := 'Unable to verify certificate revocation status'

functions.name: is_valid_hostname_format

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname to validate (domain name or IP address)

functions.description: Validates that the hostname is either a valid domain name (including mDNS .local names), IPv4 address, or IPv6 address according to DNS and IP addressing standards.
functions.algorithm_detail: Checks: (1) If domain name: matches RFC 1035 format, allows mDNS .local suffix, validates label lengths and total length. (2) If IPv4: validates dotted decimal notation, each octet 0-255. (3) If IPv6: validates hex colon notation with optional compression. Returns true if any format matches, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when configuring endpoint (Initial -> Endpoint_Configured transition) to validate hostname format before attempting connection.

functions.name: provided_port_or_default_443
functions.description: Returns the user-provided port number if specified, otherwise returns the default TLS port 443.
functions.algorithm_detail: If port parameter provided and > 0, return provided port. Else return 443 as mandated by specification for omitted port numbers.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during endpoint configuration to set the target port for TLS connection.

functions.name: validate_hostname_format
functions.description: Performs comprehensive hostname format validation and returns true if valid, false otherwise.
functions.algorithm_detail: Executes same validation as is_valid_hostname_format. Used to set state variable after validation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during endpoint configuration to record validation result in state.

functions.name: resolve_hostname_to_ip
functions.description: Resolves hostname to IP address using DNS or mDNS. Supports public DNS, mDNS (.local), and direct IP addresses.
functions.algorithm_detail: If hostname is IP address, return as-is. If domain name: query DNS servers (or mDNS for .local domains) to resolve to IPv4 or IPv6 address. Supports dual-stack resolution. May return multiple addresses; implementation selects based on network stack preference.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when initiating TLS handshake (Endpoint_Configured -> Handshake_Initiated) to determine target IP for connection.

functions.name: send_client_hello

parameters.name: cipher_suites
parameters.description: List of supported cipher suites (all PFS-enabled)

functions.description: Constructs and sends TLS ClientHello message to server initiating handshake. Includes proposed version, cipher suites, extensions, and random nonce.
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 8446: construct ClientHello with: (1) legacy_version = 0x0303 (TLS 1.2) for compatibility, actual version in supported_versions extension. (2) random = 32-byte random nonce. (3) cipher_suites = list of PFS cipher suites only (ECDHE/DHE based). (4) extensions = supported_versions (0x002b) with TLS 1.3+, key_share, signature_algorithms, etc. Send to server over TCP connection.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when initiating handshake to begin TLS negotiation process.

functions.name: extract_version
functions.description: Extracts negotiated TLS version from ServerHello message.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse ServerHello message per RFC 8446. Extract version from supported_versions extension (0x002b). If extension missing, use legacy_version field. Return version as enum (TLS_1_0, TLS_1_1, TLS_1_2, TLS_1_3, etc).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when processing ServerHello to determine negotiated protocol version for validation.

functions.name: verify_version_is_tls13_or_later
functions.description: Verifies that negotiated version is TLS 1.3 (0x0304) or later. Rejects earlier versions per specification requirement.
functions.algorithm_detail: Compare version enum value against TLS_1_3 constant. Return true if version >= TLS_1_3, false otherwise. Enforces SHALL requirement that only TLS 1.3+ is supported.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during version negotiation to enforce mandatory TLS 1.3+ requirement.

functions.name: extract_cipher_suite
functions.description: Extracts selected cipher suite from ServerHello message.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse ServerHello cipher_suite field (2 bytes). Map to cipher suite enum (e.g., TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 = 0xC02F). Return cipher suite identifier.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when processing ServerHello to determine which cipher suite server selected.

functions.name: verify_cipher_suite_provides_pfs
functions.description: Verifies that selected cipher suite provides Perfect Forward Secrecy through ephemeral key exchange.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if cipher suite uses ephemeral Diffie-Hellman (DHE) or Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman Ephemeral (ECDHE) for key exchange. TLS 1.3 cipher suites (e.g., TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256) implicitly use ECDHE/DHE. For TLS 1.2 fallback (if ever supported): parse cipher suite name for DHE or ECDHE. Return true if PFS-enabled, false for static RSA key exchange or other non-PFS methods.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during cipher suite negotiation to enforce SHALL requirement for PFS-only cipher suites.

functions.name: cipher_provides_pfs
functions.description: Predicate function checking if cipher suite provides PFS. Same as verify_cipher_suite_provides_pfs but for guard conditions.
functions.algorithm_detail: Same algorithm as verify_cipher_suite_provides_pfs.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions to determine transition path based on PFS capability.

functions.name: extract_key_exchange
functions.description: Extracts the key exchange algorithm from cipher suite (ECDHE or DHE).
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse cipher suite identifier. TLS 1.3 uses ECDHE by default (all TLS 1.3 suites). Return 'ECDHE' for TLS 1.3. For legacy compatibility: extract key exchange from suite name.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during cipher negotiation to record which key exchange algorithm will be used.

functions.name: extract_certificate
functions.description: Extracts the server's certificate (leaf certificate) from TLS Certificate message.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse TLS Certificate message per RFC 8446 Section 4.4.2. Extract certificate_list, which is a chain of DER-encoded X.509 certificates. Return first certificate in list (server's leaf certificate). Remaining certificates are intermediates/root.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when receiving server certificate to extract leaf certificate for validation.

functions.name: extract_certificate_chain
functions.description: Extracts the entire certificate chain from TLS Certificate message.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse TLS Certificate message. Extract all certificates from certificate_list. Return as ordered list: [leaf_cert, intermediate_cert_1, ..., intermediate_cert_n]. Root CA usually not included (client must have it pre-installed).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when receiving server certificate to extract full chain for chain of trust validation.

functions.name: is_x509v3_compliant
functions.description: Checks if certificate is X.509v3 compliant per RFC 5280.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse certificate version field. X.509v3 has version = 2 (0-indexed, so encoded as 0x02). Check for required v3 extensions if present: BasicConstraints, KeyUsage, ExtendedKeyUsage, SubjectAltName (for server certs). Verify certificate structure matches RFC 5280 ASN.1 schema. Return true if compliant, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during certificate format validation to enforce X.509v3 requirement.

functions.name: is_der_encoded
functions.description: Checks if certificate is encoded using Distinguished Encoding Rules (DER) per X.690.
functions.algorithm_detail: Verify certificate encoding follows DER rules: (1) Definite length encoding (not indefinite). (2) Shortest possible encoding for lengths and values. (3) No padding bits set in unused portions. (4) Unique representation (deterministic). DER is a subset of BER with strict encoding rules. Parse ASN.1 structure and verify all elements follow DER constraints.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during certificate format validation to enforce DER encoding requirement.

functions.name: verify_x509v3_compliance
functions.description: Performs comprehensive X.509v3 compliance verification. Throws exception if non-compliant.
functions.algorithm_detail: Same checks as is_x509v3_compliant but with detailed error reporting. Validates all required fields and extensions per RFC 5280.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during certificate validation to enforce compliance and capture detailed error information.

functions.name: verify_der_encoding
functions.description: Performs comprehensive DER encoding verification. Throws exception if encoding invalid.
functions.algorithm_detail: Same checks as is_der_encoded but with detailed error reporting for specific encoding violations.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during certificate validation to enforce DER encoding and capture error details.

functions.name: calculate_sha256_fingerprint
functions.description: Calculates SHA-256 fingerprint of certificate by hashing its DER-encoded binary data.
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 6234 SHA-256: Extract raw DER-encoded certificate bytes (not parsed structure, but original binary encoding). Apply SHA-256 hash function to entire certificate byte array. Return 256-bit (32-byte) hash output. This fingerprint is deterministic for any certificate and enables easy comparison/verification.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during certificate format validation to compute fingerprint for potential certificate pinning or comparison.

functions.name: chain_links_to_trusted_root

parameters.name: trusted_root_cas
parameters.description: List of trusted root CA certificates (TLSRCACs)

functions.description: Validates that certificate chain links to one of the trusted root CA certificates.
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 4158 Chain of Trust: (1) Start with leaf certificate. (2) Verify leaf is issued by first intermediate (check Issuer DN matches intermediate's Subject DN). (3) Continue up chain, verifying each cert is issued by next. (4) Terminal certificate must be issued by or be a trusted root CA in trusted_root_cas list. (5) Verify no loops or breaks in chain. Return true if valid path to trusted root exists, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during certificate chain validation to enforce trust model and authenticate server identity.

functions.name: all_signatures_valid
functions.description: Verifies that all certificates in chain have valid digital signatures.
functions.algorithm_detail: For each certificate in chain: (1) Extract signature algorithm (e.g., RSA-SHA256, ECDSA-SHA256). (2) Extract signature value. (3) Extract issuer's public key (from next cert in chain, or from trusted root). (4) Verify signature over certificate's TBSCertificate (to-be-signed portion) using issuer's public key and specified algorithm. (5) All signatures must verify successfully. Return true if all valid, false if any signature verification fails.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during certificate chain validation to ensure cryptographic integrity of certificate chain.

functions.name: verify_certificate_chain_to_root
functions.description: Performs comprehensive chain of trust validation to trusted root. Throws exception if invalid.
functions.algorithm_detail: Same as chain_links_to_trusted_root but with detailed error reporting at each step of chain validation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during certificate chain validation to enforce trust model with detailed error capture.

functions.name: verify_all_signatures_in_chain
functions.description: Verifies all signatures in chain. Throws exception if any signature invalid.
functions.algorithm_detail: Same as all_signatures_valid but with detailed error reporting for specific signature failures.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during certificate chain validation to enforce cryptographic integrity with error details.

functions.name: identify_trusted_root
functions.description: Identifies which trusted root CA issued the terminal certificate in the chain.
functions.algorithm_detail: After chain validation succeeds: Extract terminal certificate (last in chain or self-signed intermediate). Match against trusted root CA list by Subject DN and public key. Return the trusted root CA certificate that validated the chain.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after successful chain validation to record which trust anchor was used.

functions.name: extract_not_before
functions.description: Extracts the notBefore timestamp from certificate validity period.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse certificate Validity field. Extract notBefore time (UTCTime or GeneralizedTime format per X.509). Convert to system timestamp representation. Return timestamp indicating when certificate becomes valid.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during expiry validation to determine certificate start time.

functions.name: extract_not_after
functions.description: Extracts the notAfter timestamp from certificate validity period.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse certificate Validity field. Extract notAfter time. Convert to system timestamp. Return timestamp indicating when certificate expires.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during expiry validation to determine certificate end time.

functions.name: get_current_system_time
functions.description: Gets current system time for time-based validations.
functions.algorithm_detail: Query system clock or RTC. Return current time as timestamp. Assumes system clock is reasonably synchronized (within certificate validity tolerance). Critical assumption: system time is not significantly wrong or manipulated.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during certificate expiry validation to compare against certificate validity period.

functions.name: verify_certificate_time_validity
functions.description: Verifies that current time is within certificate validity period.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check: current_time >= not_before AND current_time <= not_after. Return true if certificate is currently valid, false if expired or not yet valid.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during expiry validation to enforce time-based certificate validity.

functions.name: perform_revocation_check
functions.description: Checks certificate revocation status via CRL (Certificate Revocation List) or OCSP (Online Certificate Status Protocol).
functions.algorithm_detail: Attempt revocation check in order of preference: (1) OCSP if certificate has Authority Information Access extension with OCSP responder URL: send OCSP request with certificate serial number, verify OCSP response signature, check status (good/revoked/unknown). (2) CRL if certificate has CRL Distribution Points extension: download CRL from specified URL, verify CRL signature, search for certificate serial number. (3) If neither available, revocation check cannot be performed (policy decision: fail-open or fail-closed). Return revocation status.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during certificate validation to detect revoked certificates (SHOULD requirement).

functions.name: is_certificate_revoked
functions.description: Predicate checking if certificate is revoked. Returns true if revoked, false otherwise.
functions.algorithm_detail: Calls perform_revocation_check and interprets result. Returns true only if definitively revoked. Unknown status or check failure may return false (fail-open) or true (fail-closed) depending on policy.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions to determine if revocation should abort connection.

functions.name: determine_revocation_method
functions.description: Determines which revocation checking method was used or is available.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check certificate extensions: If has Authority Information Access with OCSP URL, return OCSP. Else if has CRL Distribution Points, return CRL. Else return NONE.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during revocation check to record which method was used.

functions.name: check_ocsp_if_applicable
functions.description: Checks OCSP if applicable, returns validity status or null if not applicable.
functions.algorithm_detail: If certificate has OCSP responder URL: perform OCSP check, return true if good, false if revoked. If no OCSP available, return null.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during revocation check to attempt OCSP validation.

functions.name: check_crl_if_applicable
functions.description: Checks CRL if applicable, returns validity status or null if not applicable.
functions.algorithm_detail: If certificate has CRL Distribution Points: download and check CRL, return true if not listed, false if listed. If no CRL available, return null.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during revocation check to attempt CRL validation.

functions.name: hostname_matches_certificate
functions.description: Verifies that requested hostname matches a name in certificate (SubjectAltName or CommonName).
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 6125 hostname verification: (1) Check SubjectAltName (SAN) extension for dNSName entries. If SAN present: hostname must match one of the dNSName values (case-insensitive, supports wildcards like *.example.com). (2) If no SAN, check CommonName (CN) in Subject DN. (3) For IP addresses: match against iPAddress entries in SAN. Return true if match found, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during hostname verification to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks via certificate mismatch.

functions.name: verify_hostname_in_san_or_cn
functions.description: Performs hostname verification with detailed error reporting.
functions.algorithm_detail: Same as hostname_matches_certificate but throws exception with details if no match.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during hostname verification to enforce identity validation.

functions.name: check_subject_alt_names
functions.description: Checks if hostname matches any SubjectAltName entry.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse SAN extension, extract all dNSName entries, compare hostname (case-insensitive) with each. Support wildcard matching. Return true if match found.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during hostname verification to check SAN entries.

functions.name: check_common_name
functions.description: Checks if hostname matches CommonName in certificate Subject.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse Subject DN, extract CN attribute, compare hostname (case-insensitive) with CN value. Return true if match.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during hostname verification as fallback if no SAN extension.

functions.name: check_client_certificate_availability
functions.description: Checks if node has a client certificate and private key available for authentication.
functions.algorithm_detail: Query local certificate store or secure storage. Return true if valid client certificate and corresponding private key are available, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when server requests client certificate to determine if client can proceed with mutual authentication.

functions.name: load_client_certificate
functions.description: Loads client certificate from secure storage.
functions.algorithm_detail: Retrieve client certificate from certificate store, hardware secure element, or key management system. Return certificate object.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when preparing to send client certificate to server.

functions.name: load_client_private_key
functions.description: Loads client private key from secure storage.
functions.algorithm_detail: Retrieve client private key from secure storage (protected keystore, hardware security module, TPM, etc). Key must remain confidential. Return private key handle for signing operations.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when preparing to send client certificate to sign handshake messages.

functions.name: send_certificate_message
functions.description: Sends TLS Certificate message containing client certificate to server.
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 8446 Section 4.4.2: Construct Certificate message with certificate_request_context (from server's CertificateRequest), certificate_list (client cert and optional chain). Send to server.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during client authentication to provide client certificate to server.

functions.name: sign_handshake_with_private_key
functions.description: Signs handshake transcript with client private key to prove possession.
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 8446 Section 4.4.3 CertificateVerify: (1) Compute handshake transcript hash (all handshake messages up to but not including CertificateVerify). (2) Create signature content: prepend context string, add zeros separator, add transcript hash. (3) Sign using client private key and signature algorithm matching certificate. (4) Return signature value.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during client authentication to prove possession of private key corresponding to client certificate.

functions.name: send_certificate_verify_message
functions.description: Sends TLS CertificateVerify message containing handshake signature.
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 8446 Section 4.4.3: Construct CertificateVerify message with signature algorithm identifier and signature value. Send to server.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during client authentication to complete certificate-based authentication.

functions.name: generate_ephemeral_key_pair
functions.description: Generates ephemeral Diffie-Hellman key pair for Perfect Forward Secrecy.
functions.algorithm_detail: For ECDHE: (1) Select agreed elliptic curve (e.g., secp256r1, x25519). (2) Generate random private key (scalar). (3) Compute public key (curve point). For DHE: Generate DH parameters if not provided, generate random private exponent, compute public value. Ephemeral keys are session-specific and must be destroyed after key derivation. Return key pair.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during key exchange phase to generate ephemeral keys for PFS.

functions.name: perform_key_exchange_with_pfs
functions.description: Performs Diffie-Hellman key exchange to establish shared secret with Perfect Forward Secrecy.
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 8446 Section 7.4: (1) Exchange ephemeral public keys via KeyShare extension. (2) Compute shared secret: For ECDHE: multiply peer's public key (point) by own private key (scalar) to get shared point, extract x-coordinate. For DHE: compute peer_public^own_private mod p. (3) Return shared secret (ephemeral).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during key exchange to establish shared secret using PFS-enabled algorithm.

functions.name: derive_session_keys
functions.description: Derives session encryption/decryption keys from shared secret using HKDF.
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 8446 Section 7.1 Key Schedule: (1) Use HKDF-Extract with ephemeral shared secret and transcript hash to derive handshake secret. (2) Use HKDF-Expand to derive client_write_key, server_write_key, client_write_iv, server_write_iv. (3) Further derive application traffic secrets for post-handshake data. Uses SHA-256 or SHA-384 depending on cipher suite. Return structure containing all derived keys.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during key derivation phase to establish symmetric session keys from ephemeral key exchange.

functions.name: destroy_ephemeral_keys
functions.description: Securely destroys ephemeral key material after session key derivation to ensure Perfect Forward Secrecy.
functions.algorithm_detail: Overwrite ephemeral private keys and shared secrets with zeros or random data in memory. Clear all references. Ensures that compromise of long-term keys or session keys cannot recover past session ephemeral keys. Critical for PFS guarantee.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called immediately after session key derivation to enforce PFS by destroying ephemeral material.

functions.name: send_client_finished_message
functions.description: Sends TLS Finished message to complete handshake from client side.
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 8446 Section 4.4.4: (1) Compute verify_data = HMAC(finished_key, handshake_transcript_hash). (2) Construct Finished message with verify_data. (3) Encrypt with handshake traffic key. (4) Send to server. Server will verify and send own Finished message.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to complete TLS handshake and activate encrypted communication.

functions.name: generate_connection_id
functions.description: Generates unique identifier for this TLS connection instance.
functions.algorithm_detail: Generate random unique identifier (UUID, random bytes, or sequential with uniqueness guarantee). Used to track and reference this specific TLS connection instance.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when connection established to assign connection identifier.

functions.name: abort_handshake
functions.description: Aborts TLS handshake and closes connection due to validation failure.
functions.algorithm_detail: Send TLS Alert message to server indicating handshake failure reason (bad_certificate, handshake_failure, protocol_version, insufficient_security, etc). Close TCP connection. Clean up state.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when any validation fails to properly terminate handshake and notify server.

functions.name: get_format_error
functions.description: Returns detailed description of certificate format error.
functions.algorithm_detail: Identify specific format violation: wrong version, missing required extensions, invalid DER encoding, etc. Return error string.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when format validation fails to capture error details for logging.

functions.name: get_chain_error
functions.description: Returns detailed description of certificate chain validation error.
functions.algorithm_detail: Identify chain error: broken chain, issuer DN mismatch, signature verification failure, no path to trusted root, etc. Return error string.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when chain validation fails to capture error details.

functions.name: get_expiry_error
functions.description: Returns detailed description of certificate expiry error.
functions.algorithm_detail: Determine if certificate is expired (current_time > notAfter) or not yet valid (current_time < notBefore). Return descriptive error with timestamps.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when expiry validation fails to capture error details.

functions.name: get_revocation_details
functions.description: Returns details about certificate revocation status.
functions.algorithm_detail: Extract revocation information: revocation reason, revocation date, CRL/OCSP source. Return description.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when certificate determined to be revoked to capture revocation details.

functions.name: extract_certificate_hostnames
functions.description: Extracts all hostnames from certificate (SubjectAltName and CommonName).
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse SAN extension, extract all dNSName values. Also extract CN from Subject. Return combined list of all hostnames certificate is valid for.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when hostname verification fails to log what hostnames certificate actually contains.

functions.name: extract_alert_reason
functions.description: Extracts alert description from TLS Alert message.
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse TLS Alert message. Extract AlertDescription field (bad_certificate, certificate_revoked, certificate_expired, certificate_unknown, etc). Return as string.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when server sends alert to extract failure reason.

functions.name: generate_private_key
functions.description: Generates new cryptographic private key for CSR.
functions.algorithm_detail: Generate asymmetric key pair using secure random number generator: RSA (2048+ bits) or ECC (256+ bits). Return private key. Public key derived separately.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when creating CSR to generate key pair for certificate request.

functions.name: derive_public_key
functions.description: Derives public key from private key.
functions.algorithm_detail: For RSA: extract public exponent and modulus. For ECC: compute public point from private scalar. Return public key.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during CSR creation to derive public key for inclusion in CSR.

functions.name: create_pkcs10_csr
functions.description: Creates Certificate Signing Request in PKCS#10 format.
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 2986 PKCS#10: (1) Create CertificationRequestInfo structure with version=0, subject=entity_info (DN), subjectPKInfo=public_key, attributes (optional extensions). (2) Encode as DER. (3) Sign with private_key using signature algorithm. (4) Create CertificationRequest structure with requestInfo, signatureAlgorithm, signature. (5) Return PKCS#10 CSR.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during CSR creation to construct properly formatted certificate signing request.

functions.name: sign_csr_with_private_key
functions.description: Signs CSR with private key to prove possession.
functions.algorithm_detail: Compute signature over CertificationRequestInfo using private_key and appropriate signature algorithm (RSA-SHA256, ECDSA-SHA256, etc). Update CSR signature field. This proves entity requesting certificate possesses corresponding private key.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during CSR creation to sign the request.

functions.name: verify_csr_signature
functions.description: Verifies CSR signature to ensure it was signed by holder of private key.
functions.algorithm_detail: Extract signature from CSR. Verify signature over CertificationRequestInfo using public_key from CSR. Return true if valid, false if signature verification fails. This prevents CSRs for keys the requester doesn't possess.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after CSR creation to validate signature before submitting to CA.

functions.name: securely_store_private_key
functions.description: Stores private key in secure storage with protection from unauthorized access.
functions.algorithm_detail: Store private key in protected location: hardware secure element, TPM, encrypted keystore, OS key management system. Ensure key is not accessible to other processes or users. Apply appropriate access controls.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after key generation to protect private key material.

functions.name: is_hostname_resolvable
functions.description: Checks if hostname can be resolved to IP address via DNS or mDNS.
functions.algorithm_detail: Attempt DNS/mDNS lookup for hostname. Return true if resolution succeeds and returns at least one valid IP address, false if resolution fails (NXDOMAIN, timeout, etc).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when initiating connection to detect DNS failures early.

functions.name: send_close_notify_alert
functions.description: Sends TLS close_notify alert to peer to initiate graceful connection closure.
functions.algorithm_detail: Per RFC 8446 Section 6.1: Construct Alert message with level=warning, description=close_notify (0). Encrypt with current write key. Send to peer. This signals intention to close connection.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when initiating connection closure from Application_Data_Transmission state.

functions.name: close_tcp_connection
functions.description: Closes underlying TCP connection.
functions.algorithm_detail: Send TCP FIN to peer, complete TCP teardown handshake (FIN-ACK), close socket, release TCP resources.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during connection closure to terminate transport layer connection.

functions.name: release_connection_resources
functions.description: Releases all resources associated with TLS connection.
functions.algorithm_detail: Free memory buffers, release socket, clear connection state, remove from connection table. Clean up all allocated resources.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when connection closes (normal or error) to prevent resource leaks.

functions.name: clear_session_keys
functions.description: Securely erases all session keys from memory.
functions.algorithm_detail: Overwrite session keys (client_write_key, server_write_key, IVs, traffic secrets) with zeros or random data. Clear all key material from memory to prevent recovery.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called during connection closure to ensure keys cannot be recovered from memory.

functions.name: determine_close_reason
functions.description: Determines reason for closing connection.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check closure trigger: application request, idle timeout, protocol error, resource limits. Return descriptive reason string.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when initiating closure to log reason for troubleshooting.

functions.name: get_current_time
functions.description: Gets current system time.
functions.algorithm_detail: Query system clock, return current timestamp.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to track activity timestamps in Application_Data_Transmission state.

functions.name: identify_network_error_type
functions.description: Identifies specific type of network error that occurred.
functions.algorithm_detail: Examine error condition: DNS failure, connection timeout, connection refused, host unreachable, network unreachable, connection reset. Return specific error type.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when network error occurs to capture detailed error information.

functions.name: determine_revocation_failure_reason
functions.description: Determines why revocation check failed.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check failure cause: OCSP responder unreachable, CRL download failed, network timeout, service unavailable. Return failure reason.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when revocation check fails to log specific failure reason.

functions.name: apply_revocation_failure_policy
functions.description: Applies configured policy when revocation check fails.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check policy configuration: fail_open (proceed despite unavailable revocation check) or fail_closed (abort connection if revocation cannot be verified). Return policy decision.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when revocation service unavailable to determine whether to proceed or abort.

functions.name: log_revocation_check_failure
functions.description: Logs revocation check failure for security monitoring.
functions.algorithm_detail: Write log entry with timestamp, certificate details, failure reason, policy applied. Enable security team to track revocation service availability issues.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when proceeding despite revocation check failure to ensure incident is recorded.

definitions.definition: Cryptographic property ensuring that compromise of long-term keys does not compromise past session keys. Achieved by using ephemeral key exchange (DHE/ECDHE) where session keys are derived from ephemeral secrets that are destroyed after use.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical. Without PFS, attacker who records encrypted traffic and later obtains server private key can decrypt all historical sessions. PFS limits compromise to current session only.

definitions.definition: Temporary cryptographic key generated for a single session and destroyed after key derivation. Used in Diffie-Hellman key exchange to establish shared secret without revealing long-term keys.
definitions.security_relevance: Essential for PFS. Ephemeral keys must be cryptographically random, session-specific, and irretrievably destroyed after use to prevent retroactive decryption.

definitions.definition: Hierarchical trust model where each certificate's authenticity is verified by the certificate above it in the chain, ultimately terminating at a trusted root CA certificate. Described in RFC 4158.
definitions.security_relevance: Foundation of PKI security. Breaks in chain or improperly validated chains enable man-in-the-middle attacks via rogue certificates.

definitions.definition: Version 3 of X.509 standard for public key certificates, defined in RFC 5280. Adds extensions to v1/v2 including BasicConstraints, KeyUsage, SubjectAltName, enabling fine-grained certificate policies.
definitions.security_relevance: Extensions prevent certificate misuse (e.g., leaf cert used as CA) and enable proper hostname validation via SubjectAltName.

definitions.definition: Deterministic subset of BER (Basic Encoding Rules) for encoding ASN.1 data. Ensures unique binary representation of any data structure. Defined in X.690.
definitions.security_relevance: Deterministic encoding enables reliable fingerprint calculation and signature verification. Non-DER encodings could enable signature substitution attacks.

definitions.definition: Cryptographic hash of certificate's DER-encoded binary data, enabling easy comparison and verification. Matter mandates SHA-256 for collision resistance.
definitions.security_relevance: Used for certificate pinning. Weak hash algorithms (MD5/SHA-1) enable collision attacks where attacker generates certificate with same fingerprint.

definitions.definition: Real-time protocol for checking certificate revocation status. Client sends certificate serial number to OCSP responder, receives signed status response (good/revoked/unknown).
definitions.security_relevance: More timely than CRL for detecting compromised certificates. Responder availability and response authenticity are critical.

definitions.definition: Signed list of revoked certificate serial numbers published by CA. Periodically updated and distributed.
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents use of compromised certificates. Timelines and availability affect effectiveness. Large CRLs can have scalability issues.

definitions.definition: X.509v3 certificate extension containing alternate names for certificate subject. Used to list all valid hostnames/IPs for server certificates.
definitions.security_relevance: Primary mechanism for hostname verification in modern TLS. Missing or incorrect SAN enables certificate impersonation attacks.

definitions.definition: Public Key Cryptography Standards #10: Certification Request Syntax Standard. Defines format for Certificate Signing Requests (CSRs). RFC 2986.
definitions.security_relevance: CSR signature proves requester possesses private key corresponding to public key being certified, preventing certificate misissuance.

definitions.definition: Multi-step protocol establishing TLS connection: version negotiation, cipher suite selection, key exchange, authentication via certificates, and session key derivation. Defined in RFC 8446 for TLS 1.3.
definitions.security_relevance: Establishes secure channel parameters and authenticates parties. Any handshake weakness or validation failure compromises entire connection security.

definitions.definition: Combination of cryptographic algorithms for key exchange, authentication, encryption, and message authentication in TLS. E.g., TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256.
definitions.security_relevance: Determines session security level. Weak cipher suites (non-PFS, export-grade) enable various attacks. Matter mandates PFS-only suites.

definitions.definition: Key derivation function based on HMAC, used in TLS 1.3 for deriving session keys from shared secrets. RFC 5869.
definitions.security_relevance: Provides cryptographically strong key derivation with domain separation. Ensures derived keys are independent and secure even if inputs have limited entropy.

security_properties.description: Only TLS 1.3 or later connections are established. Earlier versions are rejected to prevent downgrade attacks and exploitation of known vulnerabilities (BEAST, CRIME, POODLE, etc).
All connections use PFS-enabled cipher suites (ECDHE/DHE). Ephemeral keys are destroyed after session key derivation, ensuring compromise of long-term keys cannot decrypt past sessions.
Server certificates are validated through complete chain of trust to a trusted root CA. All signatures in chain are cryptographically verified.
Server certificate hostname matches requested hostname (via SubjectAltName or CommonName). Prevents man-in-the-middle attacks using valid certificates for wrong domains.
All certificates are X.509v3 compliant (RFC 5280) and DER-encoded (X.690). Ensures proper extension handling and deterministic encoding for reliable validation.
Certificates are only accepted within their validity period (notBefore to notAfter). Prevents use of expired or not-yet-valid certificates.
critical_transitions: Certificate_Chain_Validated -> Certificate_Expiry_Validated (guard: current_time >= not_before && current_time <= not_after)

security_properties.description: Certificate revocation status is checked via CRL or OCSP (when enabled). Revoked certificates are rejected to prevent use of compromised credentials.
When client certificates are used, client proves possession of private key via CertificateVerify signature. Server validates before accepting client identity.
Certificate Signing Requests (PKCS#10) are signed with private key, proving requester possesses key. Prevents certificate misissuance for keys requester doesn't control.
Session keys are derived using HKDF from ephemeral shared secrets and handshake context. Ensures cryptographically strong, independent keys for encryption.
Private keys (client certificates, ephemeral keys) remain confidential. Stored securely, never transmitted, ephemeral keys destroyed after use.
TLS endpoints have valid hostname format (domain/IP) and port (default 443 if omitted). Ensures connection targets are well-formed.
Connection closure properly clears all session keys and releases resources. Prevents key material from remaining in memory after session ends.
When revocation check fails due to service unavailability, policy determines whether to fail-open (proceed) or fail-closed (abort). Critical security decision.
Network-level errors (DNS failure, connection timeout, unreachable host) are detected and handled appropriately, preventing hangs and resource leaks.
All application data transmitted after handshake is encrypted using negotiated cipher suite and derived session keys. No plaintext application data allowed.

assumptions: DER encoding is correct and deterministic

cryptographic_operations.output: Ephemeral key pair (private scalar, public point/value)
assumptions: RNG provides sufficient entropy (at least 128-bit security)
Ephemeral keys are truly random and session-specific
Discrete logarithm problem is hard (DHE) or elliptic curve discrete log is hard (ECDHE)
No man-in-the-middle in key exchange messages
HKDF is secure key derivation function
Handshake transcript hash is correct and includes all messages
Private key is secret and only known to requester
HMAC is secure message authentication code
Finished key is secret and derived from authenticated handshake

cryptographic_operations.output: Digital signature proving private key possession

security_assumptions.assumption: Cryptographic primitives (SHA-256, RSA, ECDSA, AES-GCM, HKDF) are computationally secure and provide claimed security level.
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker can break encryption, forge signatures, create hash collisions, or predict keys, completely compromising TLS security.

security_assumptions.assumption: Random number generators provide sufficient entropy (at least 128-bit security) for key generation, nonces, and ephemeral keys.
security_assumptions.if_violated: Weak randomness enables key prediction, nonce reuse, ephemeral key compromise (breaking PFS), and session hijacking.

security_assumptions.assumption: Private keys (client, server, CA) remain confidential and are not accessible through side channels, physical attacks, or software vulnerabilities.
security_assumptions.if_violated: Compromised private keys enable complete impersonation. Client key compromise: attacker impersonates client. Server key compromise: attacker impersonates server (even with PFS, current sessions compromised). CA key compromise: attacker forges arbitrary certificates.

security_assumptions.assumption: System clocks are sufficiently synchronized (within reasonable tolerance) for certificate expiry validation to be meaningful.
security_assumptions.if_violated: Nodes with incorrect clocks accept expired or not-yet-valid certificates. Attacker presents revoked certificate to node with backdated clock, or future certificate before validation infrastructure ready.

security_assumptions.assumption: Certificate Authorities properly validate identity before issuing certificates and maintain secure infrastructure.
security_assumptions.if_violated: Misissued certificates enable impersonation. Compromised CA enables forgery of trusted certificates for any identity, breaking entire PKI.

security_assumptions.assumption: Trusted root CA certificates are authentic and securely provisioned in client trust store.
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker installs rogue root CA in client trust store, issues fraudulent certificates accepted by client, enables undetectable man-in-the-middle.

security_assumptions.assumption: DNS/mDNS infrastructure provides reasonably accurate hostname resolution and is not fully compromised.
security_assumptions.if_violated: DNS poisoning redirects connections to attacker-controlled servers. Even with certificate validation, hostname manipulation can enable targeted attacks if DNS is manipulated before connection.

security_assumptions.assumption: TLS implementation (library) correctly implements RFC 8446 without critical bugs, side-channel vulnerabilities, or parsing errors.
security_assumptions.if_violated: Implementation bugs (e.g., Heartbleed, padding oracles, state machine bypasses) enable various attacks despite protocol-level security. Side-channel attacks (timing, cache) leak keys despite correct protocol.

security_assumptions.assumption: Certificate revocation infrastructure (CRL distribution points, OCSP responders) is available when revocation checking is performed.
security_assumptions.if_violated: Unavailable revocation services create fail-open (accept revoked certs) or fail-closed (reject all certs) dilemma. Fail-open enables use of compromised certificates despite revocation.

security_assumptions.assumption: Ephemeral keys are truly ephemeral - generated per-session, used once, and irretrievably destroyed after session key derivation.
security_assumptions.if_violated: Reused or inadequately destroyed ephemeral keys break Perfect Forward Secrecy. Attacker recovering ephemeral keys can decrypt corresponding sessions.

security_assumptions.assumption: Handshake messages are not modified by active attacker. TLS MAC and encryption provide integrity for application data, but handshake relies on certificate authentication.
security_assumptions.if_violated: Active man-in-the-middle can manipulate handshake (version downgrade, cipher suite manipulation) if certificate validation fails. This is why strict certificate validation is critical.

validation_checklist.notes: COMPLETE COVERAGE ACHIEVED: 28 states, 40+ transitions, 72 functions. All SHALL requirements modeled: TLS 1.3+ enforcement, PFS mandatory, X.509v3+DER, chain validation, expiry, revocation (with fail-open/fail-closed policies), hostname verification. Optional flows included: client authentication, CSR generation. Complete connection lifecycle: initialization -> handshake -> data transmission -> graceful/error closure. All error modes: version, cipher, certificate (format/chain/expiry/revocation/hostname), network (DNS/timeout/unreachable). Timers modeled: connection closure timeout (close_timeout transition). Resource cleanup: session key erasure (clear_session_keys), connection resource release. Security properties: 5 properties added covering connection closure security, revocation failure policies, network error handling, application layer encryption. Ready for ProVerif/Tamarin formal verification.
